Customer Onboarding Guide
Communication Hub

Everything you need to configure WhatsApp messaging and Zoom Phone call integration in Lifesycle — step by step.

📄 9 sections covered
⚡ WhatsApp & Phone Calls
🔒 Status: BETA
⚙️

Settings Overview

The main Settings page — your entry point to Communication Hub configuration.

📸 Settings › All configuration areas, including the Communication Hub Settings (BETA) card.

What you'll see

Communication Hub Settings card — marked BETA. This is the single entry point for configuring both WhatsApp messaging and Phone Call integration.
Each card on this page links to a different configuration section. Click the card to navigate directly.
The Settings page is accessible via the gear icon (⚙️) in the left sidebar of Lifesycle.

Steps to get there

1
Click the gear icon (⚙️) in the left sidebar to open Settings.
2
Locate the "Communication Hub Settings" card and click it to enter the configuration area.
⚠️
BETA notice: Communication Hub is currently in BETA. Some features, UI labels, and behaviours may change without prior notice.
📋

WhatsApp › Templates

The library of pre-approved message templates used for automated and manual WhatsApp communication.

📸 WhatsApp Templates — left panel list, filter tabs, and right panel preview with template details.

What you'll see

Left panel: List of all templates with status badges — Approved, Pending, Rejected, Disabled.
Filter tabs: All · Approved · Pending · Rejected · Disabled — quickly narrow the list by status.
+ New Template button (pink): Opens the template creation form.
Right panel preview: WhatsApp phone mockup, Template Details (ID, Account, Category, Language, Status, Assigned To), Full Message Text, and defined Variables.
Delete button (red): Permanently removes the selected template. This action cannot be undone.
Assigned To: Dropdown to assign a template to the Company or a specific Branch.

How to use templates

1
Go to Settings › Communication Hub › WhatsApp Templates.
2
Use the filter tabs to show only templates with the status you need.
3
Click any template in the left list to preview its content and details on the right.
4
To create a new template, click the pink "+ New Template" button and fill in the form.
5
Use the "Assigned To" dropdown to assign the template to the Company or a specific Branch.
⚠️
Approval required: Templates must be Approved by WhatsApp/Meta before they can be sent. Pending templates cannot be used yet — approval typically takes a few minutes to 24 hours.
ℹ️
Variables: Placeholders like {{1}}, {{2}} are automatically replaced with real contact data (e.g. first name, appointment date) at send time.
📱

WhatsApp › Phone Numbers

View and manage all phone numbers connected to your WhatsApp Business account.

📸 WhatsApp Phones — connected numbers with VERIFIED status, quality rating, and assignment.

What you'll see

Account ID: Displayed at the top — confirms which WhatsApp Business account these numbers belong to.
Table columns: Phone Number · Assignment Name · Assigned To (dropdown) · Status · Quality Rating.
Sync Phone Numbers button (top right): Re-fetches the number list from Meta. Use this if a newly added number is not appearing.
Status VERIFIED: The number is active and authorised to send WhatsApp messages.
Quality Rating dot: Green = high quality (low complaint/block rate). Yellow or red requires urgent action.

How to manage phone numbers

1
Click the "Phones" tab inside WhatsApp Settings to view connected numbers.
2
Use the "Assigned To" dropdown on each row to assign a number to the Company or a Branch.
3
If a phone number is missing, click "Sync Phone Numbers" to pull the latest list from Meta.
⚠️
VERIFIED required: A phone number must show VERIFIED status before it can send any WhatsApp messages. Unverified numbers are blocked by Meta.
🚨
Quality Rating alert: A red or yellow quality rating means recipients are marking your messages as spam. Review your messaging frequency and content immediately — continued poor quality can result in Meta restricting or banning the number.
🏢

WhatsApp › Account

Check your linked WhatsApp Business API account status, messaging tier, and daily limits.

📸 WhatsApp Account — account ID, TIER_2K messaging limit (2000/2000), and connection controls.

What you'll see

Account ID: Unique identifier for your Meta WhatsApp Business account.
Tier (TIER_2K): Your current messaging tier. TIER_2K allows up to 2,000 messages per day.
Daily Limit counter (e.g. 2000 / 2000): Shows messages sent vs. the daily cap. Resets every 24 hours.
"Re-Allow WhatsApp" button: Click this to reconnect if the account shows as disconnected.
"Usage Summary" button: Opens analytics showing how many messages have been sent over time.
"+ Add WhatsApp Account" button (pink): Links an additional WhatsApp Business account.

How to manage your account

1
Click the "WhatsApp Account" tab to view your account status and daily limit.
2
Monitor the Daily Limit counter — once it reaches your cap, no further messages can be sent until the next day's reset.
3
If your account appears disconnected, click "Re-Allow WhatsApp" to restore the API connection.
4
Click "Usage Summary" to review historical send volumes and spot patterns.
ℹ️
TIER_2K limit: Your account can send up to 2,000 messages per day. If you need a higher limit (e.g. TIER_10K), you must apply through Meta Business Manager — this is not configurable in Lifesycle directly.
🚨
Never ignore a disconnected account: If disconnected, all automated WhatsApp messages (reminders, confirmations, campaigns) will silently fail — clients will not receive them and no error will be shown.
💬

WhatsApp Inbox

The live messaging inbox where staff read and reply to client WhatsApp conversations in real time.

📸 WhatsApp Inbox — conversation list, live chat thread, 24-hour timer, and message input bar.

What you'll see

Phone number selector (top-left dropdown): Choose which connected number you want to send from.
"+" button: Starts a new outbound conversation with a contact.
Inbox tabs: My Inbox (assigned to you) · Branch (your branch) · Company (all conversations).
Search bar: Find a specific contact or conversation by name or number.
Conversation list: Shows contact name, number, team label, last message preview, and timestamp.
Right panel — chat thread: Full message history with timestamps and delivery status ticks (sent / delivered / read).
Message input bar: Text field · emoji picker · template button (document icon) · send button (pink arrow).
24-hour timer (e.g. "23h 7m left"): Countdown showing how long the customer-initiated messaging window remains open.

How to use the inbox

1
Navigate to Communication Hub › WhatsApp using the speech bubble icon in the left sidebar.
2
Select the correct phone number from the dropdown in the top-left.
3
Choose a tab: My Inbox for conversations assigned to you, Branch for your branch, Company for all.
4
Click any conversation in the list to open the full chat thread on the right.
5
Type your reply in the message bar and press the pink send arrow.
6
To start a new conversation, click the "+" button and select a contact.
7
To send a pre-approved template, click the document icon in the message bar and pick a template.
⚠️
24-hour window rule: You can only send free-form messages within 24 hours of the client's last inbound message. After that window expires, you must use an approved template.
⏱️
Always check the timer: If the countdown has expired, click the document icon and send a template instead of typing a free-form reply.
📞

Phone Calls › Zoom Accounts

Connect Zoom Phone accounts to Lifesycle to enable automatic call logging and recording.

📸 Phone Calls Settings › Zoom Accounts — connected accounts with status (Connected) and phone counts.

What you'll see

Search bar: Find a specific Zoom account by name or email.
Table columns: Account ID · Account Name · Account Email · Phone Number (count of phones connected) · Status · Actions.
Status Connected: Zoom account is authorised and call data is flowing into Lifesycle.
"+ Add Zoom Account" button (pink): Launches the OAuth flow to connect a new Zoom account.
Trash icon (Actions column): Permanently removes the Zoom account integration.
Tabs: "Zoom Accounts" (current view) · "Blacklist" (numbers to exclude from logging).

How to manage Zoom accounts

1
Go to Settings › Communication Hub › Phone Calls.
2
The "Zoom Accounts" tab lists all Zoom accounts integrated with Lifesycle.
3
Check the Phone Number column to see how many Zoom Phone numbers are connected per account.
4
To add a new account, click "+ Add Zoom Account" and complete the Zoom OAuth login when prompted.
5
To remove an account, click the trash icon (🗑) in the Actions column for that row.
⚠️
Status must be Connected: Only accounts with a green Connected status will have their calls logged automatically.
ℹ️
Multiple accounts: If your organisation has more than one Zoom account, each must be added separately via the OAuth flow. They appear as separate rows in the table.
👤

Phone Calls › Zoom Account Detail

Drill-down view of a Zoom account showing which Zoom users are matched to Lifesycle users.

📸 Zoom Account Detail — user matching table with Auto Matched status and Sync Zoom Users button.

What you'll see

Account header: Shows the Zoom account owner name and email for quick identification.
"Sync Zoom Users" button (top right): Refreshes the user list from Zoom — use this after adding new Zoom users.
Search bar: Find a specific Zoom user within this account.
Table columns: Zoom User (name + email) · Phone Number · Lifesycle User (editable field) · Status · Actions (trash icon).
Status Auto Matched: Lifesycle has automatically matched the Zoom user to a Lifesycle account (usually by matching email). No manual action needed.
Lifesycle User field (editable): If auto-matching fails, manually type the correct Lifesycle user to link the Zoom user to.

How to manage user matching

1
Click on a Zoom account row in the Zoom Accounts list to open its detail view.
2
Review the Status column — any user showing Auto Matched is correctly linked.
3
For users without a match, click the Lifesycle User field and type the correct user's name to create a manual link.
4
If newly added Zoom users are missing, click "Sync Zoom Users" to pull the latest list from Zoom.
5
To remove a user mapping, click the trash icon in the Actions column.
ℹ️
Auto Matching: Lifesycle matches Zoom users to Lifesycle accounts using the email address. If both share the same email, matching happens automatically with no configuration required.
⚠️
Unmatched users: Calls made by an unmatched Zoom user will not be attributed to any Lifesycle staff member — they appear as unknown in call logs. Always resolve unmatched users promptly.
🚫

Phone Calls › Blacklist

Manage phone numbers that should be excluded from call logging in Lifesycle.

📸 Phone Calls › Blacklist — numbers excluded from call logging, with Name, Phone Number, Added At columns and delete actions.

What you'll see

Blacklist tab: Located within Phone Calls settings alongside the Zoom Accounts tab.
Table columns: Name · Phone Number · Added At · Actions (trash icon to remove).
"+ Add to Blacklist" button (pink, top right): Add a new number to the exclusion list.
Trash icon: Each entry can be individually removed to restore logging for that number.

How to manage the blacklist

1
Go to Settings › Communication Hub › Phone Calls › Blacklist.
2
Review the list of currently blacklisted numbers.
3
To add a number, click "+ Add to Blacklist" and enter the details. Calls from/to that number will no longer be logged.
4
To remove a number, click the trash icon next to it — logging will resume immediately.
ℹ️
Use case: Common examples include internal office lines, test numbers, or third-party vendor numbers whose calls should not appear in client records.
⚠️
Review periodically: Blacklisted numbers are silently excluded — staff won't see any calls logged for them. Audit this list regularly to ensure real client calls are not being inadvertently excluded.
📼

Phone Calls › Call Detail

Individual call record showing metadata, AI summary, recording playback, and transcript.

📸 Call Detail — call metadata, AI-generated summary with key points and next steps, and transcript search.

What you'll see

Call metadata: Date/time, duration, direction (Outbound/Inbound), caller and recipient numbers, and the assigned Lifesycle staff member.
AI Call Summary: Automatically generated summary including Purpose of Call, Key Discussion Points, Agreed Next Steps, and Commercial Opportunity — no manual note-taking required.
Confidence indicator: Shows the AI's confidence level in the generated summary.
Recording player: Play back the call audio directly within Lifesycle. Scrub to any point in the conversation.
Transcript search: Search within the call transcript to jump to specific keywords or moments.
Contact link: If the number is matched to a Lifesycle contact, a direct link to their profile is shown.
Staff attribution: Shows which Lifesycle user handled the call, based on Zoom user matching.

How to review a call

1
Navigate to Communication Hub › Phone Calls from the left sidebar.
2
Find the call in the call log list by date, staff member, or contact name.
3
Click the call row to open the Call Detail view.
4
Read the AI Call Summary for a quick overview, or use the recording player for the full conversation.
5
Use transcript search to find a specific part of the conversation by keyword.
6
Click the contact link to jump directly to the client's profile in Lifesycle.
ℹ️
Recording availability: Recordings are available only if call recording is enabled in your Zoom Phone settings. Contact your Zoom administrator if recordings are missing.
⚠️
Staff attribution: If a call shows no staff member, the Zoom user who made the call has not been matched to a Lifesycle user. Go to Zoom Account Detail and resolve the mapping.

Lifesycle — Communication Hub Onboarding Guide · v1.0 · April 2026

For support, contact your Lifesycle account manager or visit the Help Centre.
